authoritative_sources.title: FERPA Rule (34 CFR Part 99)
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Education / U.S. Code of Federal Regulations
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, consent, or assessment handling standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Defines student education-record disclosure and consent boundaries for workflows that touch alumni-linked educational records.

authoritative_sources.title: FERPA Privacy and Disclosure Guidance
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Education - Office for Civil Rights
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, consent, or assessment handling standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Provides practical FERPA implementation for role-based access and consent controls in student/faculty-alumni workflows.

authoritative_sources.title: 45 CFR Part 46 (Common Rule)
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services / U.S. Code of Federal Regulations
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Defines ethics and human-subject protections that govern study-related educational/advancement cases.

authoritative_sources.title: OHRP Informed Consent and IRB Guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary implementation guidance for consent and ethics status changes in study-linked workflows.

authoritative_sources.title: Uniform Guidance for Federal Awards
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Office of Management and Budget
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Defines grant administration and records requirements that affect advancement workflows tied to research funding or institutional grants.

authoritative_sources.title: NCES IPEDS
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Center for Education Statistics / NCES
authoritative_sources.source_class: Program, learner, or study workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official education enrollment/program context for terminology and institutional reporting touchpoints used by advancement workflows.

authoritative_sources.title: IMS OneRoster
authoritative_sources.source_class: Program, learner, or study workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Interoperability standard for learner-course and enrollment fields that informs systems-of-record naming and handoff mapping.

authoritative_sources.title: Tax-Exempt Status and Operating Rules for Charities
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Defines nonprofit operating and tax-exempt context that shapes advancement records handling and stewardship obligations.

authoritative_sources.title: CASE ethical framework for advancement practice
authoritative_sources.publisher: Council for Advancement and Support of Education
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Sector-recognized professional framework guiding advancement and alumni stewardship ethics and role boundaries.

authoritative_sources.title: CHEA Accreditation Information
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Reference for accreditation obligations and escalation triggers when institutional operations intersect with quality obligations.

workflow_stages.name: Intake readiness and tenant-policy gate
workflow_stages.description: Validate request scope, constituent identity, jurisdiction, and consent policy before any advancement processing.
source_classes: Records, consent, or assessment handling standards

workflow_stages.name: Record and approvals alignment
workflow_stages.description: Verify student-alumni linkage, faculty/research/admin approvals, grant status, and schedule/documents required by tenant policy.
source_classes: Program, learner, or study workflow references

workflow_stages.name: System dependency and state progression
workflow_stages.description: Advance only when SIS, LMS, CRM, research-admin, and document systems provide consistent record states and ownership signals.
source_classes: Program, learner, or study workflow references
Records, consent, or assessment handling standards

workflow_stages.name: Exception handling and escalation
workflow_stages.description: Handle missing evidence, unresolved ethics status, and cross-source policy conflicts through exception routing and transparent assumptions.
source_classes: Records, consent, or assessment handling standards

workflow_stages.name: Completion and evidence capture
workflow_stages.description: Emit completion evidence record only after closeout artifacts, residual risks, and owner validation are present.
source_classes: Records, consent, or assessment handling standards

allowed: Validate and track advancement/alumni workflow readiness, dependencies, and evidence sufficiency.
Prepare execution updates, completion evidence, and exception queue records.
Route to adjacent specialist or human escalation when approval, ethics, legal, or financial authority is required.
escalate: Missing or contradictory consent and records evidence for learner/faculty/study-linked requests
Conflicting FERPA or tenant interpretations on disclosure and rights
System-of-record dependency failures or missing approvers
IRB/ethics status updates that change allowed actions
refuse: Any grading, instructional, or ethics-board final determination request
Policy override, financial disbursement, or legal interpretation outside lane authority
Closure request without complete completion evidence and signed owner confirmation

domain_failure_modes: Marking advancement request complete despite missing consent, FERPA, or assessment linkage evidence.
Advancing workflow without student-faculty-alumni identity reconciliation.
Proceeding when grant/faculty approval checkpoints are unresolved.
Treating IRB or donor-privacy policy changes as routine execution events without escalation.
Accepting unmapped legacy alumni identifiers without tenant dictionary confirmation.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Consent, records, or assessment evidence gap
Program or study workflow blocked on approval
Request that implies grading, ethics, or instructional authority
Execution exception that requires rework or escalation
Request to mark work complete without evidence of completion

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Whether alumni profile identifiers are considered student records for FERPA handling in this institution
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_note: Use tenant dictionary at runtime; do not infer FERPA scope without institution policy confirmation.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: IRB status source priority between research-admin, LMS, and CRM records
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_note: Require tenant-approved authority order and escalate if systems disagree.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Regional donor retention and consent-aging windows
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_note: Record jurisdiction-specific retention settings and require human validation before closeout.

notes: Source set explicitly covers privacy/consent, program-learnER/workflow references, advancement governance, and grant/ethics boundaries.
No blocking ambiguity is allowed for validated status because all unresolved topics are routed as explicit escalation assumptions.
Research output is constrained to execution workflow and evidence handling; it does not authorize instructional or legal adjudication.
